newsSinceJun6: No material company news since June 6. The -7.2% move on June 9 appears flow-driven: profit-taking/consolidation in a stock up ~89% YTD that already saw a post-earnings 'sell the news' fade in May (Q1 beat, no guidance, JPMorgan Neutral initiation) and now trades at the ~$43 consensus analyst target. The most recent fundamental items (Bluetooth HDT design win with a U.S. semiconductor company; strongest licensing quarter in three years) date to May 11, 2026.

sources.label: Investing.com - Why is CEVA stock sliding (post-Q1 dynamics, May 11, 2026)
StockTitan - Ceva Bluetooth HDT design win press release (May 11, 2026)
Seeking Alpha - Ceva 2026 revenue growth guidance commentary

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already recommended removal from this CPO alpha page on relevance grounds (no direct CPO/HBM/packaging/test exposure), and nothing since June 6 changes that. Today's -7.2% is flow-driven profit-taking, which does not improve page-specific asymmetry for a name whose thesis here is non-relevance; hold at the bottom pending removal.
verdicts.bull: Strongest licensing quarter in three years, growing NeuPro AI NPU traction (>20% of 2025 licensing revenue), and the first full-stack Bluetooth HDT RF design win suggest a structural mix shift toward higher-value system IP deals.
verdicts.neutral: A solid edge-IP licensing story, but it is connectivity/edge-AI IP, not co-packaged optics or AI-compute bottleneck infrastructure; after +89% YTD the stock trades at the ~$43 consensus target with the rerating largely complete.
verdicts.bear: Royalty-dependent model with declining YoY non-GAAP EPS, no forward guidance given at Q1, and a +89% YTD run leave it exposed to further multiple compression; on this page it is simply the wrong exposure for the CPO bottleneck thesis.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: New large NPU or Bluetooth-7/HDT licensing deals or customer silicon ramps converting to royalties in H2 2026.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued de-rating of a post-run-up, low-relevance name; IP licensing lumpiness and royalty adoption volatility.
